label: NS S11
phase: Hardening — Coverage Recovery & CRD Smoke Tests

hypotheses.name: 3-phase sprint works across project types
hypotheses.evidence: Hardening sprint (coverage recovery + smoke tests) executed cleanly in 12 minutes. All 9 gates passed first run. The 3-phase process handles non-feature sprints (test-only deliverables) as effectively as feature sprints.

hypotheses.name: Gates catch real issues
hypotheses.evidence: Coverage gate provided the signal that motivated this sprint — Sprint 10 dropped from 95% to 80%. After Sprint 11 hardening, coverage recovered to 92%. Gates also validated all new tests pass and lint stays clean.

hypotheses.name: Skills are followed by agents
hypotheses.evidence: 4/5 H7 instructions passed. Instruction #1 (APIs are versioned) was not applicable — no new API/CRD changes in a hardening sprint. Instructions #2-5 were not directly applicable to test-only code (no new plugins, no new error messages, no new user stories). The sprint followed production-engineer skill conventions (table-driven tests, behavior testing, mock external deps) correctly.

hypotheses.name: Coverage gate catches regressions
hypotheses.evidence: Coverage gate flagged the regression from 95% to 80% in Sprint 10, directly motivating Sprint 11's scope. Recovery to 92% validates the feedback loop: gate detects regression, next sprint prioritizes recovery.

hypotheses.name: Flowstate works for greenfield projects
hypotheses.evidence: Sprint 11 is the 12th sprint. The project has two binaries, CRD support, Helm subcharts, 72 unit tests + 10 smoke tests, and 92% coverage. Process continues to work for hardening sprints as well as feature sprints.
